prompt.text: This is the cheapest AWS model. I'm using US West 2 currently so any of the models there. Nova Micro would be OK, just a cheap model When no remote exists ask only for the URL and construct the command automatically I don't want you to determine the session ended by inactivity. I want you to only exit automatically if you get confirmation the session ended or have a command from the GG command to manually exit. They can then review what's been done so far and go from there

response.text:
Thanks — I’ll keep this read-only and give you the refined step plan with your constraints baked in.

Updated plan (specific steps, ready to delegate to agents)
1) CLI contract + command surface
- Remove any `--daemon` flag or `gg session ...` commands.
- `gg` with no args prints intro + auto-starts daemon, then exits.
- Only commands:
  - `gg status` → interactive review UI
  - `gg "<prompt>"` → Bedrock plan + HITL execution
- Unknown args are treated as natural language prompt.

2) Daemon lifecycle + auto-start
- Daemon starts only via `gg` (no flag).
- Health check via socket; auto-spawn if missing.
- Daemon owns all session detection; no CLI “session event”.

3) Session model + lifecycle (no inactivity-based end)
- Define a unified session event model from Cursor/OpenCode/Claude.
- Session “ended” only if:
  - Tool explicitly indicates end, OR
  - User explicitly issues a `gg` command to end/exit session (manual exit command).
- No inactivity heuristics.

4) Cursor ingestion (full prompt/response)
- Extend Cursor reader to fetch bubble content and emit events per assistant response.
- Map events to repo; update session model fields.

5) OpenCode ingestion
- Identify storage/schema in US West 2 environment.
- Implement event extraction per assistant response.
- Emit session end only when tool confirms.

6) Claude Code ingestion
- Identify storage/schema.
- Implement event extraction per assistant response.
- Emit session end only when tool confirms.

7) Auto-commit after each response
- On every assistant response event:
  - stage changes (respect `.gitignore` and `.ggignore` as equivalent)
  - commit with generated summary
  - write notes/refs metadata (session id, tool, model, author, etc)

8) End-of-session review UI (`gg status`)
- TUI-like list of commits:
  - Navigation: Vim (j/k, gg/G), arrows
  - Selection: space toggles
  - Actions: `s` squash, `a` amend, `u` undo all to staged
  - Enter = accept then push (if remote exists and tree clean)
- If session not ended: show “active” view + option to manually end.

9) Remote handling
- If no remote, prompt for URL only, then run `git remote add origin <URL>`.
- Push only when remote exists + working tree clean.

10) Natural language prompt mode (Bedrock)
- Use AWS Bedrock in `us-west-2`, cheapest model default = **Nova Micro**.
- Always HITL: show proposed git commands, require confirmation before executing.
- Provide override env var for model, e.g. `GG_BEDROCK_MODEL`.

Remaining clarification (only 1)
- What do you want the manual “end session” command to be?
  - Recommended: `gg status --end` (keeps the minimal command surface).

If you confirm the end-session command name, I’ll produce the per-file execution checklist for each step so agents can run independently.

meta.session_title: Plan for a Git-based autonomous CLI daemon with commit automation
